tables.title: vaBench release coverage and materialization status
tables.caption: Coverage/materialization status for the 64-entry L1/L2 release target, split into core circuit coverage and measurement/stimulus support; score denominator claims are governed by the score denominator manifest.

tables.title: EVAS/Spectre parity and rerun status
tables.caption: EVAS/Spectre parity status for certified release forms; score, speed/debug, and model baselines are separate gates.

tables.title: Allowed and blocked paper claims
tables.caption: Use only safe_wording for allowed claims; blocked claims must not be used as conclusions.

tables.title: External blockers and stop conditions
tables.caption: External blockers explain unavailable fresh Spectre evidence; they are not certification evidence.

tables.title: Speed/debug, scoring, and baseline gates
tables.caption: Speed/debug and baseline results are pending until the score denominator is enabled and same-slice evidence exists.

coverage.safe_caption_note: Coverage target only; split core circuit coverage from measurement/stimulus support; not a scored denominator.
Core analog/mixed-signal circuit-function entries; excludes measurement and stimulus/source support categories.
Auxiliary measurement, instrumentation, and stimulus/source entries; report separately from core circuit coverage.
L1 coverage count from the tracker.
L2 coverage count from the tracker.
Static certification is separate from EVAS/Spectre parity.
Score denominator contains certified content-denominator rows; content-excluded duplicates are not counted.

parity.safe_caption_note: Subset evidence only; do not phrase as full release certification.
Zero mismatch applies to the certified release forms.
Pending forms block full release certification when nonzero.
Queue readiness is execution planning, not pass evidence.
Staged bundles are runnable when the bridge is reachable.
Bridge status explains why fresh Spectre evidence is pending.
Historical main120 evidence supports development history, not the release denominator.
Historical main120 evidence supports development history, not the release denominator.

claim_gate.safe_wording: The current release package defines 64 planned L1/L2 entries; this is a coverage target, not a final scored benchmark result.
The release has 64 materialized entries for the 64-entry plan and 219 static-certified forms with zero asset issues.
On the imported certified subset (217 forms), EVAS PASS / Spectre FAIL count is 0.
This claim is blocked until every release form has current EVAS/Spectre evidence with zero dual failures and zero EVAS PASS / Spectre FAIL mismatches.
claim_gate.blocked_until: resolve 2 dual-pending forms; complete fresh rerun for 2 queued forms (includes 0 historical source-equivalence blockers); bridge diagnostics must report a ready profile; fresh dual rerun summary must import successfully

claim_gate.safe_wording: The release benchmark score denominator is enabled for 51 certified content-denominator entries and 184 forms.
Speed/debug has subset timing evidence, but release-wide speedup remains blocked until the dedicated artifact marks the speed claim allowed.
claim_gate.blocked_until: Run same-slice EVAS/Spectre timing for every scored release form, or state a narrower subset-only claim.; Stratify or fix EVAS slow outliers before claiming aggregate speedup on the release package.; Keep per-row EVAS and Spectre wall-clock timings from run_gold_dual_suite timing fields.; Keep Spectre-reported runtime from spectre.out alongside wrapper wall-clock timings.; Report machine/bridge/Cadence configuration with the timing artifact.; Do not compute speedup from historical main120 summaries because they lack same-slice runtime metadata.

claim_gate.safe_wording: Model baseline reporting is pending until baseline runs report against the enabled score denominator.
claim_gate.blocked_until: baseline runs must report against counted release entries/forms only

claim_gate.safe_wording: L0 conformance has 4 cases and contributes 0 entries to benchmark coverage.
The package structure and source assets are ready, but full completion remains blocked by fresh dual rerun, scoring, speed, and baseline gates.
claim_gate.blocked_until: selected EVAS/Spectre rerun pending; external blocker report active: 0 blocked, 1 pending; speed/debug timing artifact not claimable; release model baseline artifact pending

external_blockers.diagnosis: dry run: no bridge tunnel or simulator command was executed
external_blockers.claim_impact: release package cannot be claimed fully EVAS/Spectre certified; score denominator remains disabled
external_blockers.stop_condition: The active rerun summary has status=complete, zero expected misses, and is imported into dual_certification.json.

external_blockers.diagnosis: downstream paper claims remain disabled until their dedicated artifacts are explicitly enabled
external_blockers.claim_impact: do not claim EVAS speedup/debug advantage; do not claim model baseline results on the release package
external_blockers.stop_condition: paper_artifacts claim gates allow scored benchmark, speedup, and baseline claims only after their dedicated artifacts support them.

speed_baseline.current_value: Timing exists, but at least one rerun row did not PASS.
speed_baseline.required_before_claim: Run same-slice EVAS/Spectre timing for every scored release form, or state a narrower subset-only claim.; Stratify or fix EVAS slow outliers before claiming aggregate speedup on the release package.; Keep per-row EVAS and Spectre wall-clock timings from run_gold_dual_suite timing fields.; Keep Spectre-reported runtime from spectre.out alongside wrapper wall-clock timings.; Report machine/bridge/Cadence configuration with the timing artifact.; Do not compute speedup from historical main120 summaries because they lack same-slice runtime metadata.

Use the enabled score_denominator_manifest.json as the denominator source of truth.; Run baseline model outputs through the release evaluator on scored rows.; Publish pass@1 / axis rates / failure taxonomy with Spectre as the final judge.; Keep baseline methods simple and describe them as stress tests, not as the paper's core method.
benchmark_score must be explicitly enabled for counted entries/forms

claim_boundary: These tables are presentation artifacts; they do not create new certification evidence.
Parity rows must be captioned according to whether they cover the full release or only an imported subset.
Rows with blocked claim_status must not be used as paper conclusions.
